description_long: This triumphal arch is a monumental structure featuring a large central archway flanked by two massive piers, each adorned with three tiers of recessed panels. The panels are decorated with relief-like motifs, suggesting classical or commemorative ornamentation. The structure is robust and symmetrical, with a flat entablature crowning the top. The archway is wide and tall enough to allow passage for people, vehicles, or processions, typical of ceremonial arches found in historic city centers. The exterior appears to be constructed from stone or concrete, giving it a solid, enduring presence. Subtle decorative details, such as the repeated arched niches and possible sculptural reliefs, enhance its grandeur and historical character.
description: A grand, stone-like triumphal arch with three tiers of decorative panels and a large central passageway, featuring classical relief motifs and a flat top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main archway and three tiers of decorative panels with relief motifs.
description_view_1: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, with the same archway and panel arrangement.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the depth of the arch and the continuous decorative panels along the sides.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the other side, with visible arch depth and panel details.
